evaluations.prompt: Design a small lending-library API whose loans persist and whose overdue notices run after the request. Use Koan's native application language.
requiredSignals: uses Entity<T> and standard Entity operations
uses Jobs for Entity-owned background work
proposes focused behavior, composition, and corrective proof

evaluations.prompt: Migrate an ASP.NET Core reminders app to Koan while preserving its routes, payloads, MongoDB database, container topology, and environment keys. It currently has repositories, hosted polling, and handwritten CRUD controllers.
requiredSignals: classifies mechanisms as keep, absorb, rebuild, or delete
moves persistence and CRUD to Entity<T> surfaces
moves background work only after naming its guarantee
migrates and proves one mechanism at a time

evaluations.prompt: A team wants durable Jobs but references only the Jobs package and no durable Data provider. Explain the implementation and expected failure behavior.
requiredSignals: distinguishes in-memory convenience from durable restart survival
requires a durable Data provider for the durability claim
expects a corrective composition failure for explicitly required durability
